channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Riot Games Announces LEC Rivals Challenge To Earn Fans Prizes
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has announced the LEC Rivals Challenge in which fans can support their favourite esports team by playing games of League of Legends in hopes of meeting the players.

Participants will earn points for playing LoL matches and climb leaderboards, both individually and for their selected team, with various prizes on offer.

During the LEC Spring Split, esports fans can take part in the challenge on Riot’s FTW platform, with meet-and-greet opportunities with players, LEC tickets, gaming hardware, and a year of having every LoL skin and champion unlocked as possible prizes.

The challenge will start on March 28 and end on June 6. It will only be open to players on the EUN1, EUW1, ME1, TR1, and RU servers and to players in 135 select countries.

How to Earn Points in LEC Rivals

All the following objectives are repeatable and available in games of Normal (Draft pick), Ranked, Normal (Swiftplay), Arena, ARAM, Normal, and Ranked Flex.

  • Play and win a game of LoL – 3 points
  • Play and finish a game – 1 point
  • Destroy a turret – 2 points
  • Takedown a Dragon – 2 points
  • Takedown a Baron – 3 points
  • Earn a penta kill – 10 points

There will also be additional limited-time objectives. These special objectives can earn players points, contribute points to their team, or unlock multipliers.

Some special objectives may require chosen teams’ fans to collectively meet a certain target to unlock points. In such cases, only the team that reaches the target first will earn those points.

LEC Rivals Prizes

LEC Rivals will feature leaderboards and prizes both for overall, individual play and for the highest-scoring fans of each LEC team.

The team prizes vary for each team. For example, G2 Esports offers a game of ARAM with its players as a top prize, whereas Fnatic offers a tour of its Berlin office.

Surprisingly, there aren’t any in-game customisation prizes like team icons

The prizes available are as follows:

  • Team Leaderboard
    • Top 1: VIP Experience with the team and a signed jersey
    • Top 2-5: Signed jersey (or similar)
    • Top 6-10: Team merch
    • Top 11-15: Smaller team merch items
  • Individual Leaderboard
    • Top 1: Omen 16 League of Legends Limited Edition Laptop + LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year
    • Top 2: LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year + 35 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 3: LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year + 30 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 4: LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year + 28 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 5: LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year + 26 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 6: 24 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 7: 23 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 8: 22 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 9: 21 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 10: 20 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 11-50: 12 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 51-100: 8 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 101-200: 4 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 201-500: 2 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 500-1000: 1 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 1001-5000: 1 Hextech Chest

What other ways should Riot be supporting LEC teams and fans? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

It’s suddenly looking like a good time to be an LEC fan again as Riot confirmed the LEC is staying in its physical Berlin location next year.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Strands #750 Hints and Answers for Today, March 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #750 for Monday, March 23, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 23, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #750 are all terms that have become the generic way to describe a specific item, despite being a unique branding term at first. For example: Kleenex, Photoshop, and Coke.

General Strands Hints For March 23

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: The father figure on the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
  • Hint 2: When someone finally loses control of their anger, it’s said they do this.
  • Hint 3: Don’t step on these, you’ll break your mother’s back.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #750 for March 23, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 23, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • TRAP
  • CURE
  • HATTER
  • HATER
  • HATE

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #750 On March 23, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • CRACK
  • SNAP
  • RUPTURE
  • FRACTURE
  • SHATTER
  • SPLINTER
  • SPANAGRAM: BREAKDOWN

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #750?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 24 will be Strands Game #751.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1739: Hints and Answer for March 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1739 for March 24, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, March 24, 2026

The Wordle answer for game #1739 is easier than the last. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 4 out of 6 guesses. That makes it over a full guess easier than the 5.5-guess average from March 23.

General Wordle Hints For March 24

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: This word can refer to both a group and a state of mind.
  • Hint 2: It’s often used in nature-related or emotional contexts.
  • Hint 3: The tone can feel quiet or inward, rather than active.
  • Hint 4: You’re more likely to see this word in descriptive or narrative writing.
  • Hint 5: The name of a faction from the late 1990s WWF

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1739 for March 24, 2026.

Starter Words For March 24, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • ROBOT
  • BROAD
  • BROOM

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1739 On March 24, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: BROOD

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

BROOD can be used as a noun, a verb, or an adjective. As a noun, it means “a number of young produced or hatched at one time; a family of offspring or young.” When used as a verb, it means “to sit upon eggs to be hatched, as a bird.” Lastly, as an adjective, it means “kept for breeding.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1738 on March 23, 2026

Answer: SERIF

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1739?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on March 24 will be Wordle Game #1740.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Red Storm Was Working On At Least 10 Games At Time Of Mass Layoffs
item.content:encoded:
On March 19, Insider Gaming learned that Ubisoft was ending game development at Red Storm Entertainment. A studio nearly 30 years old, Red Storm Entertainment is responsible for creating legendary franchises like Rainbow Six and Ghost Recon.

Since 2000, the studio has been owned by Ubisoft, where it continued to develop Rainbow Six and Ghost Recon games up until the early 2010s. Since then, it primarily worked on VR games and was developing The Division Heartland—a free-to-play spinoff game in The Division series—before that was canceled.

But aside from the games that were publicly known, the studio was working on at least 10 different projects at the time of the announced layoffs at Ubisoft.

As revealed during the latest episode of Insider Gaming Weekly, Red Storm was a studio that was being used when it was really needed by Ubisoft. That said, it was needed quite a lot, and the number of projects it had been working on backed that up.

At the time of its closure, Red Storm was working on the following projects for Ubisoft:

  • Rainbow Six Siege’s seasonal content
  • The next mainline Ghost Recon game, currently known as Project OVR
  • Brawlhalla
  • Beyond Good & Evil 2
  • A smaller-scale Rainbow Six game called Slice & Dice
  • Splinter Cell
  • Audio for The Division 2
  • Conceptualization for The Division 3
  • Support for Watch Dogs Director’s Cut
  • Another unannounced project that’s currently in the conception stage

With the gaming division shut down, Red Storm is now being used as a global IT and Snowdrop support studio. It’s also expected that Ubisoft will be announcing even more cost-cutting moves in the coming weeks and months.

What are your thoughts on Red Storm having its game development ended, with over 100 people losing their jobs as a result of the layoffs by Ubisoft? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about what Capcom said regarding AI and how it plans to use it moving forward. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…
